what_it_does: This file is a channel-memory attachment record that links a board item to a channel memory entry using a board:// URI and records a creation timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no body content
standouts: target uses a board:// URI scheme (board://item-4a75a73d)
record is purely metadata with no payload
conventions: References an external object via a URI-style target
Leaves the body empty when no prose is needed
concerns: Target board item is opaque; no title or context is stored locally
No payload or reason for the attachment is provided
No author or editor provenance beyond the timestamp
notes: A bare, timestamped metadata stub that points a channel-memory attachment at a specific board item.
questions: What board item does item-4a75a73d represent?
What content or event does this attachment capture?
Why is there no payload or note body?
purpose_vs_actual: The file acts as an attachment record and does exactly that; it links to a board item and records when it was added.
intention_signals: It appears intended to create a durable, timestamped association between a channel memory entry and a board item so the relationship can be retrieved and audited later.
novelty: It uses a board:// URI scheme as the attachment target rather than a file path or code symbol, and carries no body content.
contribution: Contributes a typed, timestamped edge between a channel-memory attachment and a board item, preserving the existence and direction of the link for fusion and provenance.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a channel-memory attachment record (att-10419faa) in the dragnet-development channel that references board item item-4a75a73d via a board:// URI and carries an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to persist a link between a channel memory entry and a board item for later retrieval and audit.
